name: Node Local Scratch Staging
summary: Stage data through node-local scratch with guarded cleanup.
description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm pattern for using node-local temporary storage, including capacity evidence, secure job-local directory creation, stage-in and stage-out logs, and marker-guarded cleanup.

tools.name: sbatch
tools.notes: Required only when submitting the example as a Slurm job.

tools.name: rm
tools.notes: Used only by marker-guarded cleanup when CLEANUP_NODE_LOCAL=1 or CONFIRM_DELETE=1.

inputs.name: input_manifest
inputs.description: Newline-delimited list of files or directories to stage into node-local scratch.

inputs.name: node_local_base
inputs.description: Base directory for node-local temporary storage, often TMPDIR, SLURM_TMPDIR, or a site-specific path.

inputs.name: output_dir
inputs.description: Durable user-owned directory where selected outputs and logs are staged out.

inputs.name: workload_command
inputs.description: Optional command to run from the node-local working directory after stage-in.

outputs.name: node_local_report
outputs.description: Capacity, environment, stage-in, workload, stage-out, and cleanup evidence for the job-local directory.

outputs.name: staged_inputs
outputs.description: Inputs copied into a secure job-local working directory.

outputs.name: staged_outputs
outputs.description: Selected outputs copied back to durable storage.

outputs.name: cleanup_record
outputs.description: Evidence that cleanup was skipped, completed, or refused by marker checks.

examples.title: Clean up a marked node-local run directory
Review site policy before using node-local scratch

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check shell syntax for the staging wrapper and cleanup helper.
Run with a tiny manifest in a short allocation and confirm outputs stage out before any cleanup is enabled.
